title: Planner
description: Hybrid selection (ADR 0003): the code guarantees, the LLM decides. Owns: deterministic pool composition (weeknight active_max pool + weekend pool, veg-floor top-up, untested injection — all code-side policy), a single LLM selection call over cheap-tier candidates with signals-by-mechanism (structured fields as data, household context + kid-friendliness as prose), the WeekPlan structured output (== working_plan; per-meal veg path inherent/separable/second_dish; day always null in v1.0), deterministic post-validation (counts, pool membership, veg consistency, no duplicates, flag sanity) with ONE bounded repair retry, and get_recipe enrich of the chosen. Depends on E2. Source: ADR 0003, SPEC §6.
close_reason: Epic complete — all children closed (8zs.1–8zs.12: hybrid selection contract, WeekPlan schema, validate+repair, enrich, quality-aware multi-seed retrieval, shape-repair retry, untested-from-pool enforcement, pool-sufficiency pre-check, side-dish pairing). Planner hardened and feature-complete for v1.x.

title: Slack Posting & Draft Rendering
description: Outbound-only Slack for v1.0 (SPEC §7, §9.2). Owns: the new Slack app in the Backhaushold workspace (bot token, chat:write scope only — NO Socket Mode / app-level token in v1.0), posting the weekly draft via chat.postMessage as a new top-level message (thread-per-week), a deterministic WeekPlan→Slack-markdown render step (selection ≠ presentation, ADR 0003 D3), and capturing/persisting the parent thread ts (load-bearing for v3.0). Target channel resolved by explicit ID from the dev/prod profile (never a name lookup). Called by E3's generateForWeek. Source: SPEC §7, §9.2, ADR 0003 D3.
close_reason: Epic complete: all children (bj1.1 app+channels, bj1.2 render, bj1.3 post+ts capture) closed; outbound-only Slack posting live in production runtime since PR #2.

title: Orchestrator: State, Idempotency & Recovery
description: The daemon's core loop and durable state (ADR 0002). Owns: SQLite session schema (keyed by week_key), the state machine (generating→suggested→under_revision→committed, +failed +expired), week_key computation (currentPlanWeek: clock-derived anchor Sunday in pinned TZ), the generation idempotency gate, write-before-post ordering (insert 'generating' BEFORE Slack post; update to 'suggested'+thread_ts+working_plan AFTER ts returns), startup catch-up (three cases; stale 'generating' → alert-once-then-failed, never auto-repost), expired piggybacked on next generation, and the cost-counter columns (turn_count/token_spend/cost_usd). Calls E4 (buildPlan) then E5 (post). Source: ADR 0002, SPEC §8.
close_reason: Epic complete — all children closed (bd6.1–bd6.14: state machine, idempotency, startup catch-up, resume, boot-path containment, watchdog, session-DB backup+migrations, schema SoT, daemon nits). v1.0 orchestrator hardened and shipped.

title: Recipe MCP Server
description: Custom Apple Notes MCP server + harness that we own (ADR 0001). Owns: local vector index, an ingest-time LLM extraction pass gated on content-hash + extractor_version, a cached structured-field store, the frozen ingredient capture schema (raw always kept; name/prep/quantity(range|none)/unit/optional/alternatives/group/confidence), and the two-tier tool interface — search_recipes (cheap fan-out; hard predicates active_max + veg_status, fail-closed on null/low-confidence time) and get_recipe (full incl. ingredient block, chosen only). Consumed by the Planner (E4). Ingredient block built in v1.0, fully consumed in v4.0. Source: ADR 0001, SPEC §5.
close_reason: Epic complete — all children closed (q95.1–q95.17: NoteStore reader, embedder, search, extraction, sync, tag guard, extraction backoff, fail-closed, stale-recipe reconciliation, body cap). v1.0 Recipe MCP server hardened and shipped.

title: Eliminate .env-not-loaded and vitest-worktree-glob gotchas
description: Two documented CLAUDE.md ops gotchas are workarounds for missing automation; fix at the root instead of relying on the model to remember. (1) vitest.config.ts has no exclude, so a repo-root vitest run globs .claude/worktrees/** and doubles test/file counts. Add configDefaults.exclude + '.claude/worktrees/**'. (2) .env is not auto-loaded before pnpm dev/sync; wire --env-file-if-exists=.env into the dev/sync scripts (Node 22+/tsx support it; tolerates missing .env in CI/launchd). Then remove the two now-obsolete gotcha bullets from CLAUDE.md.
close_reason: Merged in PR #41 (be015f8); .env auto-load + vitest worktree exclude + /resync-recipes skill

title: Untested-flag consistency hard-fails generation when LLM omits a derivable flag — derive it from ground-truth pool quality instead
description: Found during go-live launchd dry-run 2026-07-20. generateForWeek FAILED (PlanValidationError, even after the single ADR-0003 D5 repair) because the LLM selected an untested-quality recipe (p10587, chickpea linguine) but did NOT add the 'untested' flag; the repair attempt also didn't add it. Result: prod row -> 'failed', NO post, and startup catch-up will NOT auto-retry the week (no auto-repost by design) -> a MISSED WEEK until a manual operator re-run. The SAME recipe was correctly flagged 'untested' in 2 prior runs, so this is non-deterministic LLM omission. Fail-closed (no bad post) is correct, but a hard generation failure on a DERIVABLE presentation flag is a real reliability gap on the weekly critical path. The 'untested' flag is derivable from the chosen candidate's quality==='untested' — which 8zs.11 validation ALREADY treats as ground truth — so requiring the LLM to echo it and rejecting when it doesn't is brittle.
design: In enrich (or a normalization pass before validate), SET/force the 'untested' flag from the selected pool candidate's quality==='untested' rather than trusting the LLM to emit it. Then 8zs.11's flag-consistency check becomes a tautology sourced from ground truth on both sides. Review other derivable presentation flags (e.g. vegetarian) for the same treatment — normalize derivable metadata from ground truth, keep validation as a backstop for genuinely LLM-owned fields (slot placement, pairing choice). This converts a hard failure into automatic reconciliation and removes a class of missed-week failures.
close_reason: Fixed + merged via PR #40 (53fd915); untested flag now reconciled from ground-truth pool quality before validation, 641 tests green.

title: Dev MP_FIRE_ON_START collides with startup catch-up (UNIQUE session.week_key) — spurious 'test-fire failed' alert on a successful run
description: Found during go-live §5 verification 2026-07-20. On a dev daemon boot that is PAST the weekly trigger with no existing row: (1) onStartup catch-up generates the plan (inserts row, posts, -> suggested) — CORRECT; then (2) fireOnStart (MP_FIRE_ON_START=1) fires onTrigger again for the SAME week_key. In dev, profile forceRegenerate=true, so generateForWeek bypasses the idempotency gate and does a plain store.insert, which collides with the row catch-up just wrote -> 'UNIQUE constraint failed: session.week_key'. This is caught (daemon keeps running, no duplicate post, row intact at suggested), but it emits an #agent-alert 'Startup test-fire (fireOnStart) failed ... manual re-run may be required' — a scary FAILURE alert even though the plan generated+posted fine via catch-up. This is the exact MP_FIRE_ON_START=1 flow RUNBOOK §5/§8 tell operators to run, so the misleading alert undermines go-live confidence. PROD is unaffected (forceRegenerate=false -> fireOnStart hits the idempotency gate and skips cleanly). generate.ts intentionally makes force+existing-row throw (clean overwrite is rerun.ts's job, bd6.6) — so the real defect is the fireOnStart<>catch-up OVERLAP in dev, not generate.ts.
design: Options: (a) fireOnStart routes through the operator force-overwrite path (rerun.ts, delete-then-generate) instead of a plain force-insert; (b) skip fireOnStart if catch-up already generated for the active week this boot (make the test-fire idempotency-aware); (c) downgrade the dev force+existing-row collision from an ERROR alert to an informational 'already generated this boot; skipping test-fire' note. Prefer (b) or (c) — the test-fire is redundant once catch-up has posted.
close_reason: Fixed: fireOnStart neutralizes force for the one same-week onTrigger after catch-up this boot, so generateForWeek's idempotency gate skips cleanly instead of PK-colliding. No more spurious 'test-fire failed' alert on the RUNBOOK §5/§8 dev flow. PR #42 merged, CI green, 644 tests.

title: No per-LLM-call timeout: a single Agent SDK call storm hangs the run silently to the 45-min watchdog
description: Found during go-live verification 2026-07-20. AgentSdkLlmClient.runQuery just 'for await's the SDK query() stream with NO per-call timeout. During a transient API rough patch (observed ~08:15-08:27 EDT), the SDK subprocess kept reconnecting/retrying and never yielded a terminal message, so runQuery never resolved and never threw — buildPlan/sync hung. token_spend stayed 0 (no billable turn). Nothing reached stdout or the #agent-alerts log during the hang. The ONLY backstop is the 45-min MP_TRIGGER_TIMEOUT_MS watchdog (bd6.11), so a stalled call silently burns up to 45 min before the run fails. HARDENING: wrap each runQuery in a bounded per-call timeout (config, e.g. 3-5 min) that throws a typed LlmTimeoutError -> surfaces via the existing failure/alert path, and kills the SDK subprocess. Distinguishes 'model is slow' from 'call is wedged' far sooner than the whole-trigger watchdog.
design: Per-call timeout << trigger watchdog. On timeout: abort the SDK query, throw LlmTimeoutError (record any partial usage per fkg.9), propagate to generateForWeek's failed+alert path. Consider a small bounded retry for transient stalls before failing.
close_reason: Fixed: per-LLM-call timeout MP_LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS (default 240s, << 45-min trigger watchdog) wraps runQuery, aborts the wedged SDK subprocess, throws typed LlmTimeoutError, records partial usage, and surfaces via the existing failed+#agent-alert path. No more silent 45-min hangs on a wedged call. PR #43 merged, CI green, 649 tests.

title: Daemon inline pre-generation sync can exceed the weekly trigger budget on mass hash-invalidation
description: Found during go-live verification 2026-07-20. makeBuildPlanWithSync runs runSync INLINE before every generation. Sync is normally cheap (hash-gated -> ~$0), but when the stored index is stale en masse — e.g. a note-reader change moves the embed/body hash for most notes (exactly what commit #20/q95.16 did: 694/764 recipes went stale), or a long outage lets Notes drift — sync must re-embed + re-extract N recipes SEQUENTIALLY (~10-34s LLM each). For ~694 recipes that is a few hours and ~$14 inline, which (a) trips the $2 generationDollarCap after ~100 recipes -> CostCapExceededError aborts the run, and/or (b) blows past the 45-min trigger watchdog (bd6.11) -> the week's plan FAILS. The daemon cannot self-heal this; it needs a one-time out-of-band 'pnpm sync' with a raised cap. HARDENING: detect a large backfill BEFORE running it inline (count stale notes), and either (i) alert to #agent-alerts + skip inline sync (plan against the existing index) rather than fail the week, or (ii) telemeter 'sync will re-process N recipes (~$X, ~Ymin)'. Silent multi-hour inline sync that fails the watchdog is the worst outcome.
design: Options: pre-count stale via embeddableTextHash diff before the loop; threshold (e.g. >50 stale) -> alert + proceed-without-sync, or cap-bounded partial sync + alert to finish out-of-band. Must not let a mass re-extraction run silently inside onTrigger.
close_reason: Fixed: makeBuildPlanWithSync now cheaply pre-counts stale notes (hash-only, no embed/LLM) before the inline sync loop. Above MP_STALE_SYNC_THRESHOLD (default 50) it skips inline sync, alerts #agent-alerts with count/est-cost/est-time + /resync-recipes (RUNBOOK §6) guidance, and proceeds against the existing index — degrading gracefully instead of tripping the $2 cap or 45-min watchdog and failing the week. Below threshold: unchanged. PR #44 merged, CI green, 663 tests.

title: DECISION: AnyList integration feasibility — no official API; record the risk and a fallback target
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. SPEC 3.2 treats AnyList like Todoist (adopt a community MCP or build), but AnyList has NO official public API — only reverse-engineered community libraries that can break at any time. That is a viability risk for the entire v4.0 commit step and it is not recorded as a risk anywhere, nor is a fallback named.
design: Proposed: a short recon spike on the current community options (protocol stability, auth), then either accept with eyes open or pick a fallback commit target (Apple Reminders, a Todoist grocery project, or Slack-only list). Amend SPEC 10.5 with the outcome.
notes: Cheap to decide early — it may change whether v4.0 is worth its current shape.

title: DECISION: v4.0 grocery reconciliation mechanism (unit conversion, canonicalization, pantry staples) + package_size default
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. Everything defers reconciliation to v4.0 and SPEC 5.1 asserts aggregation becomes near-deterministic, but no mechanism is recorded anywhere: deterministic unit-conversion tables vs an LLM pass, ingredient-name canonicalization, prep-form conversion (2 cloves + 1 tbsp minced), the pantry-staple exclusion list, and the package_size capture question (9yf closed defaulting to raw — the v4.0 consumption side is unspecified). SPEC 10.6 records only the edge cases as open, not the mechanism.
design: Proposed: decide the mechanism split (deterministic tables for unit math + a bounded LLM pass for canonicalization/prep-form, consistent with the hybrid pattern in ADR-0003), the pantry-staple list ownership (config vs tags), and how raw package_size strings are consumed.
notes: Ratify before v4.0 scoping; capture side (ADR-0001 D4) is already frozen and sufficient.

title: DECISION: cost-cap pause/resume semantics for the v3.0 revision loop
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. SPEC 9.3's cap numbers are concrete (150K/cycle, 25 turns, $5/thread) but the aftermath is not: does generation spend count against the thread budget; can a paused-for-cost thread be resumed/reset by an operator; does /mealplan-approved still work while paused; is the pause a session state or just a note? SPEC calls the runaway revision loop the real risk — the recovery half of the enforcement needs a decision.
design: Proposed: amend SPEC 9.3 (or the revision ADR) with budget composition (generation + revision share the thread budget or not), an operator reset affordance, and approval-while-paused behavior (recommend: approval always allowed — it costs nothing and ends the loop).
notes: Pairs with the revision-protocol decision.

title: DECISION: v3.0 revision-loop protocol — reply-to-mutation semantics and revision/approval concurrency
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. ADR-0002 names the under_revision state but nothing specifies the mechanics: does a thread reply trigger full re-selection or a targeted swap; is the revised plan re-validated (ADR-0003 D5) and re-posted as a new thread message; how are multiple rapid family replies batched/debounced; and what happens when /mealplan-approved arrives while a revision cycle is in flight (which working_plan version commits?). These are exactly the races the build-the-daemon-early investment was meant to serve.
design: Proposed: a revision-protocol ADR covering mutation semantics, re-validation + re-post format, debounce/batching, and revision-vs-approval serialization on the session row (likely: approval reads the row's current working_plan under the same transition discipline).
notes: Gates v3.0 Socket Mode work. SPEC 10.2 (approval governance) is tracked separately and remains open.

title: DECISION: weather-to-planner contract — PlannerInput shape, horizon, degradation, and ordering vs day assignment
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. SPEC 6.5 names three derived weather signals but no contract exists: ADR-0003 PlannerInput has current_season only (no weather field), the forecast horizon is undefined, and the precipitation signal (suppress grill if rain) is inherently PER-DAY — which requires day assignment to exist first, so the selection-vs-assignment ordering with weather is unresolved. Open-Meteo fetch-failure behavior (proceed without weather vs fail run) is also unstated.
design: Proposed: amend ADR-0003 PlannerInput with a weather block (shape, horizon, degrade-to-absent on fetch failure), and specify whether weather feeds selection, day assignment, or both (depends on the day-assignment decision).
notes: Depends on the day-assignment decision for the per-day signals.
close_reason: Ratified as ADR-0003 A1 amendment (weather-to-planner), committed to main. One week-level temperature_band (cold/mild/hot) soft signal from Open-Meteo; refines tag-based current_season (no double-count); rides the single selection+assignment call (ADR-0005) — no ordering question, no per-day array; degrade-to-absent seasonal-only + SILENT (no alert) on fetch failure, never fails the week; no validate() change (advisory only). Precipitation/grill signal CUT FROM SCOPE (did-not-implement) — no structured outdoor signal to act on. Amended ADR-0003 in place per user (not new ADR). SPEC §6.5 rewritten + §11 log reconciled.

title: DECISION: Todoist data model + v2.0/v3.0 phase-ordering hole (recency reads what nothing writes yet)
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. Three unrecorded decisions: (a) what a committed meal task looks like (project, title format, due date from day, extra task for second_dish, embedded recipe id?); (b) how resolve-recent-meals-to-recipe-ids works when tasks are free text — semantic dedup depends on this mapping; (c) soft-commit re-commit/overwrite requires remembering created Todoist task ids, but the ADR-0002 session schema has no column for them. PLUS an ordering hole: v2.0 recency reads Todoist completed tasks while nothing writes meal tasks until v3.0's commit — as phased, v2.0 dedup has an empty source of truth unless the family hand-creates tasks, which is stated nowhere.
design: Proposed: one decision covering task schema + recipe-id round-trip + task-id persistence (session schema addition, coordinate with bd6.13 migrations), and an explicit resolution of the ordering (swap recency to v3.x, or document the manual-task interim as the v2.0 data source).
notes: SPEC 10.1's open question covers the completion signal only — not the task schema or the ordering.
close_reason: Ratified as ADR-0006 (docs/adr-0006-todoist-commit-recency-contract.md), committed to main. D1: recency+dedup+completion-signal MOVED v2.0->v3.x (depend on commit write). D2: commit stamps mp:rid=<recipe_id> in task description -> deterministic round-trip (wired exclude_ids + semantic penalty), no title guessing. D3: task schema (config project, recipe-title content, ISO-day due, second_dish/prep -> own tasks). D4: task-ids persist per-meal in working_plan JSON (todoist_task_id?, additive/resume-safe, no new SQL column) for re-commit update-in-place. D5: completion signal (§10.1) + MCP adopt-vs-build stay open (v3.x). SPEC phase map/§6.3/§10.1/§11/diagram reconciled. New ADR per user.

title: DECISION: day-assignment transition contract — the nullable day field has carriers but no semantics
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. The docs carefully carry a nullable day through every v1.0 schema so v2.0 is purely additive, but never specify: who assigns days (same LLM selection call vs a separate deterministic assignment pass), the value format (weekday name vs ISO date), how slot_type constrains day (must relaxed land on Sat/Sun?), how validate() extends, and how the render changes. Without this, v2.0 implementers must invent the core contract the nullable field was supposed to enable.
design: Proposed: amend ADR-0003 (or fold into ADR-0004) with the assignment mechanism, day value format, validation rules (slot/day consistency), and render changes. Interacts with the weather decision (per-day precipitation needs assignment to exist).
notes: Also interacts with bd6.13: adding day to stored working_plan is the first schema evolution the resume path must survive (see bd6.10).
close_reason: Ratified as ADR-0005 (docs/adr-0005-v2.0-day-assignment.md), committed to main. Assignment folds into the single selection call (assigns ISO-date day by placing meals onto ADR-0004 NightSchedule); day = nullable ISO date (legacy/degraded null, fresh plans validated non-null); validate() gains valid-night/slot<->weekday/QUICK-capacity-fit/distinct-days/prep-before-serve under existing D5 repair; render becomes day-ordered w/ capacity+prep annotations; first additive working_plan evolution (resume-safe, bd6.10/bd6.13). SPEC §6.1 + §11 reconciled. New ADR (not amend ADR-0003) per user.

title: DECISION: v2.0 calendar contract — source, event classification, cook-night derivation, prep timing (ADR-0004)
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. SPEC 4's v2.0 row says the system classifies events by effect on cooking capacity and points to section 10 — but 10.4 records only the calendar SOURCE question (Google vs iCloud). The bulk of v2.0 has no recorded design: what busy-day means, how events map to cooking capacity, how the calendar-derived cook-night count replaces the static 4+2 slot config, and how do-ahead prep timing is scheduled. Currently a dangling cross-reference in the SPEC.
design: Proposed: write ADR-0004 covering (a) calendar source + access mechanism, (b) event classification rules (which events reduce capacity, thresholds), (c) cook-night derivation replacing static slots, (d) prep-timing scheduling, (e) failure/degradation (no calendar -> fall back to static 4+2). Fix the SPEC 4 -> 10 dangling reference.
notes: Gates all v2.0 calendar work. Ratify before v2.0 scoping.
close_reason: Ratified as ADR-0004 (docs/adr-0004-v2.0-calendar-contract.md), committed to main. Source=local Calendar.app/EventKit (multi-iCloud aggregation, not CalDAV/Google); include-list cook/logistics roles; cooking-window overlap -> FULL/QUICK/NONE; NightSchedule product (seam for 824); derived slot counts replace static 4+2; full prep placement in v2.0 materialized to Todoist in v3.0; degrade-to-static+alert on no calendar. SPEC §4->§10 dangling ref fixed, §10.4/§10.5 calendar half + §11 log reconciled.

title: Sync design docs and runbook to shipped reality (greenfield claims, plist, ratified decisions, rotation procedure)
description: From docs review 2026-07-18, all verified against the tree. (1) CLAUDE.md:65 and AGENTS.md:20 still declare the project greenfield with no code/package.json — reality is a full src/ tree, pnpm+vitest+biome, 480 tests; CLAUDE.md Build-and-Test says record commands 'once package.json exists'. (2) RUNBOOK.md:276 says no plist is checked in — deploy/launchd/com.backhaus.meal-planner.plist + README exist; hand-authoring per RUNBOOK diverges from the committed one. (3) Three ratified decisions never backported: ADR-0001 D1 still says extraction produces effort/season/quality (tags are now authoritative, q95.11/q95.12); ADR-0001 Open Items still lists the confidence threshold (ratified 0.5, b7n, commit 6b1d73d); ADR-0003 Open Items still lists seed-query strategy (multi-seed shipped, l7x/kd5, commit e10eb31); RUNBOOK:171-182 still calls b7n/kd5/l7x open decisions. (4) RUNBOOK secret rotation covers only the 1P service account — add rotate-in-vault -> restart daemon -> verify-with-dev-dry-run for the Slack/Anthropic secrets themselves (secrets load once at boot).
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md status + real install/build/test/lint/single-test commands; RUNBOOK 7 points at deploy/launchd; ADR-0001/0003 + RUNBOOK 4 open items marked ratified with values and bead refs; RUNBOOK rotation procedure added. Per CLAUDE.md: keep ADRs and SPEC in sync when a decision changes.
notes: Pure docs; no code. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md are independent files — mirror the edits.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #22: corrected greenfield claims in CLAUDE/AGENTS, real command block, plist reference, backported ratified decisions (b7n 0.5, l7x/kd5 multi-seed, q95.11/12 tags-authoritative) to ADR-0001/0003+RUNBOOK, added Slack/Anthropic secret rotation procedure. CI green.

title: Session-DB backup story + schema-migration mechanism before the first additive schema change
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. The session DB is the permanent historical record and the v3.0 ts->week reverse map (ADR-0002: rows retained, never cleaned up), yet no backup is specified anywhere — losing it loses thread_ts (SPEC's own deaf-bot failure). And no schema-versioning/migration mechanism exists in code or docs, while v2.0 (day assignment) and v3.0 (Todoist task ids) both plan additive schema changes; bd6.10 shows a live row that fails a stricter parse after an upgrade crash-loops boot, so migrations and resume-tolerance are coupled.
design:
PROPOSAL (drafted 2026-07-18 — NOT ratified; awaiting review).

RECOMMENDATION: (a) back up the session DB with better-sqlite3's online .backup() API — one copy immediately before any migration, plus a rolling boot-time timestamped copy; (b) add PRAGMA user_version + a tiny forward-only migration runner in the SessionStore constructor; (c) make the working_plan read schema LENIENT (drop .strict(), new fields optional/nullable) so additive changes never break resume.

(a) BACKUP. Recipe index is regenerable via `pnpm sync` → only needs the ad-hoc .bak it has (gitignore it). The SESSION DB is irreplaceable (thread_ts reverse map, ADR-0002 D5). SessionStore uses WAL, so a plain cp can capture a torn state; use db.backup(dest) (WAL-consistent hot copy). Do a PRE-MIGRATION backup (mandatory — highest corruption risk) plus a rolling boot copy to data/backups/session-<ts>.sqlite, retain N. Time Machine is a backstop, not a substitute. HUMAN DECISION: retention count + cadence.

(b) VERSIONING. Treat today's initSchema() as baseline v1; stamp user_version=1 on fresh create. A migrations.ts holds an ordered Array<{to:number; run(db)}>; runner reads user_version, applies each pending fn and bumps user_version in the SAME transaction, in the ctor BEFORE initSchema/state-machine access. v2.0 nullable `day` + v3.0 Todoist ids become ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN migrations. No framework.

(c) working_plan JSON (coupled decision). Fragility = EnrichedWeekPlanSchema.strict() in resume.ts: any added field fails safeParse; bd6.10 makes onStartup ALERT+CONTINUE (contained, but the live week's plan is LOST). Fix: drop .strict() (or .passthrough()) + new fields .optional()/.nullable() → old blob (missing new field) AND new blob under old binary both parse; plan survives an additive upgrade mid-week. Reserve migrate-on-read for genuine renames/breaks; skip versioned blobs. FLAG: `day` should be nullable in the schema, matching the column.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH (order): 1. src/orchestrator/migrations.ts (runner+list). 2. session-store.ts: call runner in ctor before initSchema; add .backup() helper. 3. Boot backup wiring in src/index.ts before `new SessionStore`. 4. Relax resume.ts schema. 5. .gitignore data/backups/ + *.bak.

NOTE: implementable once ratified. Blocks v2.0 session-schema work. To proceed: ratify, then dispatch an implementation subagent.
acceptance_criteria: RUNBOOK backup section (session DB backup cadence/mechanism; note recipe index is regenerable via pnpm sync so only ad-hoc); a schema-version pragma (user_version) + minimal forward-migration runner landed BEFORE the first v2.0 schema change; decision recorded on how live-row working_plan JSON survives schema evolution (lenient parse / migrate-on-read).
notes:
RATIFIED 2026-07-18 by Jonathan: proceed with the drafted proposal. Backup retention = KEEP-LAST-8 boot copies + one mandatory PRE-MIGRATION copy. working_plan schema = lenient (drop .strict(), new fields optional/nullable). Implementation in progress.

(prior) Blocks any v2.0 session-schema work. data/recipe-index.sqlite.bak exists ad hoc today — formalize or delete.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #25 (ratified): WAL-consistent .backup() rolling keep-last-8 + mandatory pre-migration copy at boot; PRAGMA user_version forward-only migration runner in SessionStore ctor (baseline v1, empty list); lenient .passthrough() resume schema + nullable day so additive schema changes don't lose the live plan; RUNBOOK §10 + .gitignore. Verified 518 tests + CI green.

title: External dead-man switch: a dead daemon is currently silent by design
description: From docs review 2026-07-18. SPEC 9.4 decided no-heartbeat-in-Slack, but nothing external watches the daemon: startup catch-up only helps if the process starts, and the alert channel lives inside the thing that died. If launchd fails, the Mac sleeps (OS update reverting pmset), or secrets expire at boot, the daemon can be down for weeks with no post and no alert. An external dead-man switch was never considered anywhere in SPEC/RUNBOOK.
design:
PROPOSAL (drafted 2026-07-18 — NOT ratified; awaiting review).

RECOMMENDATION: Option (i) — a weekly ping to a healthchecks.io-style dead-man service on genuine trigger success. It is the only option whose watcher lives OUTSIDE the daemon's failure domain, which is the whole point of the bead.

(i) EXTERNAL DEAD-MAN PING. On each successful weekly trigger the daemon pings a random check URL; the SERVICE alerts (email/SMS) from its side if a ping is missed. Correct failure domain: fires precisely when the Mac is asleep, launchd is broken, or secrets expired at boot — the silent-death cases SPEC §9.4 leaves uncovered. PRIVACY: only an opaque UUID URL leaves the machine — no recipe/household data (treat URL as a mild secret). SETUP: create one check (weekly period + grace), set an env var. Does NOT contradict no-heartbeat-in-#agent-alerts (§9.4) — watcher is deliberately external.

(ii) "NO-SESSION-ROW-BY-MONDAY" LOCAL CHECK. A separate launchd StartCalendarInterval job reads the session DB and alerts on a missing current-week row. Fully local, no third party — BUT shares the daemon's failure domain: if the Mac is off/asleep or launchd is broken, the checker doesn't run either, and its alert needs the same Slack secret that may have expired. Catches "ran but skipped a week," not "daemon/host is dead." Weaker for the stated threat.

THIRD OPTION (cron on a DIFFERENT machine) is more robust than (ii) but more setup than (i); for a single family Mac, (i) wins on robustness-per-effort.

INTEGRATION POINT: composeDaemon's onTrigger (compose.ts), after boundGenerate(...) resolves WITHOUT throwing — genuine success is "generated" OR "skipped" (both mean the scheduler fired and host is alive). A failed generation throws (contained by croner catch); the missed ping then correctly triggers the external alert, complementing the internal one. Ping must be BEST-EFFORT: try/catch, short fetch timeout, never rethrow. HUMAN SETS UP: the check + the URL.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH: 1. MP_HEALTHCHECK_URL (optional; unset=disabled) in config.ts (MP_/optional* pattern). 2. src/daemon/heartbeat.ts: makeHeartbeat(url) -> never-throwing ()=>Promise<void> (timed fetch, catch-all, log-only). 3. Thread optional heartbeat dep into ComposeDaemonDeps, call at end of onTrigger. 4. Build in index.ts, pass to composeDaemon. FLAG: optionally also POST healthchecks /fail on a caught failure — decide whether that usefully duplicates the internal alert.

NOTE: implementable once ratified + you create the external check. To proceed: ratify, then dispatch an implementation subagent.
acceptance_criteria: Pick and implement a minimal liveness check: e.g. weekly ping to a free healthchecks.io-style service on successful trigger completion (alert fires from THEIR side on missed ping), or a no-session-row-by-Monday external check. RUNBOOK notes the boot-failure-is-silent risk and the chosen mitigation.
notes: RATIFIED 2026-07-18 by Jonathan: Option (i) external healthchecks.io-style dead-man ping. Include the /fail POST on a caught generation failure (defense-in-depth). Code lands feature-flagged (MP_HEALTHCHECK_URL unset = disabled); human ops step (create the check + set the URL) is a follow-up. Implementation in progress.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #26 (ratified Option i + /fail): never-throwing best-effort healthchecks.io-style heartbeat pinged from onTrigger (success->base, caught failure->/fail); MP_HEALTHCHECK_URL optional (disabled by default); coordinator-wired into index.ts; RUNBOOK §11 setup. External check+URL is a follow-up ops step. Verified 531 tests + CI green.

title: macOS TCC/Automation + Full Disk Access: document grant/re-verify procedure and alert loudly on denied/empty Notes reads
description: From docs+robustness review 2026-07-18. Reading Notes via osascript requires a TCC Automation grant to the invoking process, and reading NoteStore.sqlite requires Full Disk Access; under launchd there is no UI to answer the prompt, so a fresh machine, a new binary path, or a macOS upgrade resetting TCC turns every sync into a silent or hanging no-op (a blocked permission dialog is also a hang vector for bd6.11). Neither RUNBOOK prerequisites nor SPEC ops mention granting or re-verifying these.
acceptance_criteria: RUNBOOK gains: TCC/FDA grant procedure for the daemon's node binary, a post-OS-update re-verify step, and the Apple-schema-change risk note (Notes AppleScript interface / NoteStore schema can change across macOS versions). Code: sync path alerts loudly (not warn-only) on a denied or implausibly-empty Notes read.
notes: Same failure vector family as q95.13 (NoteStore read) and bd6.11 (osascript hang).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #28: SyncResult.suspiciousEmptyRead signal (from q95.14 guard) triggers a loud secret-free #agent-alerts alert in makeBuildPlanWithSync (proceed+alert); RUNBOOK §0.1-0.3 TCC/FDA grant + post-OS-update re-verify + Apple-schema-change risk. Verified 546 tests + CI green.

title: stripHtml applied to plaintext note bodies silently deletes angle-bracket spans before hash/embed/extract
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. notes-reader.ts:238-243 runs stripHtml over note.body, but body is JXA plaintext() output (script at :150-190), not HTML — the docstring at :124-127 is stale. The tag regex ([^>]* matches newlines) excises everything between a literal < and the next > — plausible in recipes: simmer <10 min, keep temp < 300F followed lines later by > 2 cups flour. The corruption happens BEFORE contentHash, so it is stable and never self-heals; it feeds wrong ingredient/time text to extraction and the embedding, and also decodes literal &-style text the user typed.
acceptance_criteria: readNotes stops calling stripHtml (bodies already plaintext); stale docstring fixed; regression test with angle-bracket body text; consider a one-time re-embed/re-extract of affected notes (hash changes automatically once stripping is removed, so a normal sync heals the corpus — verify).
notes: Severity is likelihood-limited but the corruption is silent and permanent; the fix is a deletion.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #20: removed erroneous stripHtml over already-plaintext note bodies (was corrupting angle-bracket spans pre-hash); self-heals on next sync via hash change. Verified 488 tests + CI green.

title: 'At most one untested meal' enforced only via the LLM's own flags — unflagged untested selections pass validation
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. validate.ts:190-203 counts the untested FLAG the model emitted, and :139-147 checks only flag=>quality, never quality=>flag. If the model selects two or three quality:untested candidates and simply omits the flag, validation passes: the week is dominated by never-cooked recipes and the draft does not even mark them try-this. Exactly the verified-not-trusted class ADR-0003 D5 exists for — the ground truth (candidate quality from the pool) is already available to the validator.
acceptance_criteria: Validation derives untested-ness from the pool candidate's quality field: the <=1 count uses pool quality, and flag consistency requires the untested flag WHENEVER the candidate is untested (both directions). Tests: unflagged-untested selection is rejected/repaired.
notes: Repair prompt already exists; this only strengthens the deterministic check.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #36: untested cap + both-direction flag consistency now derive untested-ness from the pool candidate's quality (ground truth), not the LLM flag; unflagged-untested picks now rejected/repaired. 8zs.10 shape-repair untouched. Verified 587 tests + CI green.

title: Malformed selection response (shape failure) gets no repair retry — asymmetric with semantic validation and extraction
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. validate.ts:296-325 explicitly does not catch/repair a shape failure; select.ts:112-135 throws PlanSelectionError on strict WeekPlanSchema rejection and never retries — while extraction.ts:149-188 DOES repair shape/parse failures once. So a week_plan envelope wrapper, a string "null" day, or one extra key fails the run immediately with the ADR-0003 D5 repair budget unused. Shape drift is the most common and most repairable LLM failure mode; the family loses the week's plan over it.
acceptance_criteria: selectValidatedPlan catches PlanSelectionError from the first call and spends the single bounded retry on a shape-repair prompt (same pattern as extraction.ts); total LLM calls still <= 2 (initial + one repair, shared between shape and semantic failure); tests cover envelope/extra-key/parse-failure repair.
notes: ADR-0003 D5 allots the retry for failure generally, not only semantic failure.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #35: selectValidatedPlan now catches initial PlanSelectionError and spends the single shared repair budget on a shape-repair prompt (mirrors extraction); shape XOR semantic, <=2 LLM calls, enforced by call-count tests. Verified 584 tests + CI green.

title: Tag-rated recipe with failed extraction (fields:null) passes weekend-pool filters, then getRecipe null kills the whole run
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. With fields===null, search.ts:127-187 lets main_dinner_only, quality:rated, and season all pass via tags, and the weekend pool applies no active_max (pools.ts:101-105) — so a #4stars recipe whose extraction failed (needsReview/fields:null record; guaranteed to exist transiently under q95.7) surfaces as a plausible candidate (time unknown, veg_status unknown) and can be selected. get-recipe.ts:60-64 then returns null for it (the doc comment at :40-46 wrongly claims filtered candidates by definition had successful extraction), and enrich.ts:58-67 throws EnrichmentError — failing the run AFTER pool composition and both LLM calls were paid.
acceptance_criteria: Fail closed: either any filtered search drops candidates with fields===null, or pool composition excludes fields:null records (implicit requires-extraction predicate on quality-filtered searches). Fix the stale get-recipe doc comment. Test: needsReview record with rating tags never reaches a pool.
notes: Interacts with q95.7 (retry/backoff) — until that lands, failed-extraction records are common after any bulk sync hiccup.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #24: fail-closed guard in search.ts passesFilters drops fields===null candidates from filtered queries (tag signal can't smuggle a failed-extraction recipe into a pool); get-recipe doc corrected. Verified 499 tests + CI green.

title: Notes deleted from or moved out of the Food folder are never removed from the index — stale recipes plannable forever
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. sync.ts:122-190 iterates current notes only; vector-store.ts and structured-store.ts have no delete API. A deleted note (or one moved out of Food — the exact scenario behind the drinks purge, commit fa2714e) survives indefinitely: vector row, body, structured fields, tags all remain, so the dead recipe keeps ranking in search_recipes, can be selected into a plan, and get_recipe returns it. Contradicts ADR-0001 single-owner-of-index-freshness.
acceptance_criteria: After the note loop, diff stored ids (listIds() on both stores) against ids just read; delete or tombstone (active flag honored by search) absent rows. GUARD: a suspicious empty/tiny read (0 notes while the store holds hundreds) must NOT wipe the index (folder-rename protection) — skip reconciliation and warn instead. Tests cover delete, move-out, and empty-read guard.
notes: Empty-read guard doubles as defense-in-depth for the q95.13 class of failure.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #27: post-loop reconciliation hard-deletes notes absent from the sync read (deleted/moved out of Food folder) from both stores; empty-read guard (0 read + non-empty store) skips+warns to prevent index wipe on folder-rename/permission-loss; SyncResult.removed added. Verified 543 tests + CI green.

title: Contain boot-path failures: catch-up rethrow, D4 alert-before-transition, and fireOnStart failure all crash boot
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. Three same-pattern containment gaps, all asymmetric with the scheduled-fire path (which croner catch contains). (1) startup.ts:79 — a startup catch-up generateForWeek failure rethrows (generate.ts:237 rethrows even after marking failed + alerting) -> boot crash; if the throw is from store.insert itself (disk full, SQLITE_BUSY) no row is written, so every KeepAlive restart re-runs full boot incl. sync/LLM spend: crash loop with alert spam and repeated cost. (2) startup.ts:97-100 — D4 stale-generating path alerts BEFORE the undefended transition-to-failed; a store fault re-alerts and crashes boot on every restart, violating alert-once. (3) daemon.ts:82-84 — a fireOnStart test-fire failure rejects runDaemon -> exit(1); with MP_FIRE_ON_START persisted in launchd env plus dev forceRegenerate, each restart re-fires into the PK-insert throw (bd6.7 force+existing-row hole): tight restart loop.
acceptance_criteria: (1) startup catch-up: contain generateForWeek throw (log + continue; it already alerted/recorded). (2) D4: transition first or try/catch the transition (log + continue; re-alert-next-restart is the acceptable degraded mode). (3) test-fire failure caught and logged/alerted, daemon keeps running. Tests for each.
notes: Same class as bd6.7 items but distinct call sites (startup/daemon, not generate.ts).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #21: contained catch-up generate throw (log+continue), D4 transition-first+wrapped, and fireOnStart failure (log+alert+keep-running). generate.ts untouched. Verified 491 tests + CI green.

title: Scope recipe sync to the food-only Notes folder + purge already-indexed drinks
description: The Apple Notes 'Recipes' folder is a SMART folder aggregating 'Food' (764) and 'Drinks' (34); syncing it pulled 34 drinks (e.g. 'Christmas Mimosas!') into the recipe index, where they pass main_dinner_only (untagged notes are main_dinner_eligible=true) and get planned as dinners. Fix = target the food-only real folder instead. JXA flattens the folder tree, so 'Food' is directly addressable by bare name -> config-only, no reader code change. Supersedes the course-allow-list idea (requiring #dinner) since scoping the source folder is cleaner and cheaper.
acceptance_criteria: MP_RECIPES_FOLDER points at the food-only folder ('Food'); the 34 drink rows are removed from recipe-index.sqlite (count 798 -> 764); a this-week dry-run selects no beverages; .env.example + docs/RUNBOOK.md document that the folder must be the food-only source, not the aggregating smart folder.
notes: Real tree: Shared/Recipes 1/{Food:764, Drinks:34}; 'Recipes'(798) is the smart folder. Purge mechanism: stores have NO delete method (sync only upserts) -> one-off transactional SQL delete across notes/vec_notes/structured_fields, backed up first. FOLLOW-UP (separate issue): sync should prune notes no longer in the target folder / deleted from Notes, so stale rows don't linger.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 (squash-merged to main). Recipe sync now targets the food-only 'Food' folder (DEFAULT_RECIPES_FOLDER + .env + docs); the 34 already-indexed drinks were purged from recipe-index.sqlite (798->764); this-week dry-run confirmed zero beverages selected.

title: Clean up 1Password secrets config: dedicated Meal-Planner vault + kebab-case naming + fresh OPSA
description:
Reorganize the meal-planner secrets in 1Password onto a clean, self-contained convention. Currently the items live in a shared vault with a 'Meal Planner' name prefix and non-kebab field names, and the service-account token was exposed in a terminal session (see notes), so it must be rotated. Rolling the rotation into a proper vault/naming cleanup avoids touching secrets twice.

Scope:
1. Create a DEDICATED '\''Meal-Planner'\'' 1Password vault (isolates these secrets; the vault name provides the namespace).
2. Rename items to kebab-case WITHOUT the '\''Meal Planner'\'' prefix (redundant now that they live in a dedicated vault) — e.g. '\''anthropic-api-key'\'', '\''slack-bot-token'\''.
3. Rename fields to kebab-case.
4. Create a NEW OPSA (1Password Service Account) scoped to ONLY the '\''Meal-Planner'\'' vault (least-privilege), replacing the exposed token. Revoke the old OPSA.
5. Update the op:// references in the .env (MP_OP_ANTHROPIC_KEY_REF, MP_OP_SLACK_TOKEN_REF) and OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN to match the new vault/item/field paths, and update docs/RUNBOOK.md's secrets section accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: '\''Meal-Planner'\'' vault exists holding kebab-case items (no name prefix) with kebab-case fields; a new least-privilege OPSA scoped to that vault is issued and the old (exposed) token revoked; .env op:// refs + OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN updated and a boot (pnpm sync or daemon) successfully loads secrets via the new refs; docs/RUNBOOK.md secrets section reflects the new naming.
notes: Context: the OLD OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN was accidentally printed to a terminal session on 2026-07-16 (shell-quoting mistake during a status check), so rotation is required regardless of the naming cleanup — this chore bundles the two so secrets are touched once. loadSecrets() (src/secrets/secrets.ts) reads MP_OP_*_REF via 'op read' on the 1Password path (auto-selected when OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN is set); only the op:// ref STRINGS change, no code change expected. Verify the new OPSA has read access to the new vault before revoking the old one.
close_reason: 1Password secrets cleaned up + OPSA rotated. Dedicated 'Meal-Planner' vault with kebab-case items (anthropic-api, slack-app) and 'credential' fields; new service account scoped read-only to only that vault (old exposed OPSA revoked by user). New op:// refs verified live via 'op read' (non-empty, values not printed). RUNBOOK + .env.example updated (PR #13, squash-merged).

title: Wire v1.0 tag-based seasonality: derive current season from the clock and feed the planner
description: v1.0 tag-based seasonality is specified (SPEC §116/§151/§204, ADR-0003 §60/§104/§116-118) but only HALF-BUILT and untracked: the consumption side exists (search_recipes 'season' hard filter; the selection prompt's 'respect current season' soft bias; pools.ts threads cfg.season/current_season), but nothing ever SETS it. There is no 'season' field on Config and no derivation of the current season from the date/timezone, so cfg.season/current_season are always undefined and seasonality is dormant. Observed live 2026-07-16: 'Chicken Khao Soi' (tagged winter/fall/spring, NOT summer) was selected for a July week because season played no role — it survived on quality(5)+veg+variety alone. pools.ts even carries a comment: 'season isn't yet a field on Config (no current-season signal is wired up).' Scope: derive one of {spring,summer,fall,winter} from the current date in the configured MP_TIMEZONE, thread it into buildPlanFor's cfg (index.ts), matching the user's four season tags (#spring/#summer/#fall/#winter). Live weather (Open-Meteo) is the SEPARATE v2.0 layer (SPEC §236) — do not double-count.
design:
APPROVED DESIGN (2026-07-16 brainstorm). Three-part surgical change; season is DERIVED, never configured (no new env/config/schema).

1. New pure unit src/planner/season.ts: type Season = spring|summer|fall|winter; seasonForDate(date, timezone): reads month IN the IANA zone via Intl.DateTimeFormat({timeZone, month:'numeric'}), maps meteorological (3-5 spring, 6-8 summer, 9-11 fall, 12/1/2 winter). Returns the union (assignable to existing season?: string fields -> NO type ripple; keep fields as string per user).

2. Wiring src/index.ts rawBuildPlan (~line 294): add cfg.season = seasonForDate(new Date(), config.timezone). main() is composition root that owns the real clock (matches nowDate: () => new Date()). Derived once per real generation (past idempotency gate) from the WALL CLOCK at generation time (user chose clock over weekKey). buildPlan already flows cfg.season into BOTH composePools (hard filter) and buildPlannerInput currentSeason (soft bias) -- no other wiring.

3. Bug fix src/recipe-mcp/search.ts (~145-148): drop condition must be untagged-EXEMPT. Drop iff filters.season!==undefined && effectiveSeasons.length>0 && !effectiveSeasons.includes(filters.season). Untagged (empty season_tags) -> always eligible; tagged-not-now (e.g. Khao Soi winter/fall/spring in July) -> dropped; in-season -> retained+soft-boosted.

DECISIONS: (a) enforcement = hard-exclude out-of-season + soft bias, untagged exempt; (b) month->season = meteorological/month-based; (c) untagged always eligible. Corpus evidence: 100 recipes, 54 season-tagged, only 21 summer -> hard filter WITHOUT untagged-exempt would collapse pool to ~21 and drop year-round staples.

TESTS (TDD): season.test.ts (12 months, TZ-boundary UTC instant landing in different Chicago month, DST-adjacent); search.test.ts (untagged retained under filter, out-of-season dropped, in-season retained); build-plan.test.ts (derived season reaches both search filters and current_season).
acceptance_criteria: Current season is derived from the clock in MP_TIMEZONE and passed to the planner; a plan in a given season prefers/keeps season-tagged recipes and avoids off-season ones (e.g. no winter-only soup selected in July). Design decisions resolved: month->season mapping, and hard-filter vs soft-bias (note: hard-filter thins a small corpus and raises veg-floor pressure).
notes: Design/brainstorm before implementing: (1) month->season boundaries; (2) hard search 'season' filter vs soft current_season prompt bias vs both; (3) whether untagged-season recipes are always eligible (recommended, to avoid over-thinning). Removing them shrinks the prompt (cost win) and removes a now-unused inference. title: Type-check test files in CI/typecheck (tests currently excluded from tsc)
description: Surfaced during resume-quietly review (bd6.5): tsconfig.json excludes src/**/*.test.ts from tsc, and vitest.config.ts has no typecheck block — so test files are NEVER type-checked. This let a real type error slip (a resumeQuietly returning ActiveSession assigned to an incompatible OnStartupDeps signature 'passed' because the test wasn't type-checked). Add test typechecking: either a separate tsconfig (e.g. tsconfig.test.json including tests) run in a 'typecheck:test' script, or enable Vitest's typecheck mode (vitest --typecheck). Wire it into the acceptance gates so future type errors in tests are caught. Medium priority — improves the whole SDD loop's rigor.

title: Cap/backoff for permanently-failing note extraction + tighten sync failure handling
description: Follow-up from extraction review (q95.3, commit 9e1767b). (1) MODERATE: a note whose extraction deterministically fails re-attempts (1 initial + 1 repair = 2 LLM calls) on EVERY sync forever, no backoff/cap — because needs_review is a staleness OR-condition and the failed record rewrites content_hash to current. Add a per-note attempt cap or backoff (e.g. stop retrying after N consecutive failures until the body changes) to bound cost/noise. Does NOT cascade into the embed gate (independence holds). (2) MINOR: sync.ts failure try/catch wraps both extractRecipeFields AND the success-path upsertStructured — a DB write error after a SUCCESSFUL extraction is mislabeled as an extraction failure, discarding a good result; tighten the try to just the extraction call. (3) MINOR: the catch discards the error entirely — log a redacted reason (error.message is already proven secret-free) for triage. Non-blocking; extraction runs ~weekly.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #23: per-note failed_attempts counter + MAX_EXTRACTION_ATTEMPTS=3 cap (parks failing notes until body changes), tightened try to extraction-only, redacted-reason logging. fkg.6 cap-abort + embed gate preserved. Verified 497 tests + CI green.

title: Surface per-turn LLM error messages in the SDK harness
description: Follow-up from the Agent SDK harness review (7js.5, commit 42f09c9). The adapter only throws on a non-success FINAL result message; per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error values (authentication_failed, billing_error, rate_limit, overloaded, server_error) are not inspected — a turn could error yet still contribute usage and let the loop reach a 'success' result, producing silent partial failures that are hard to debug. Inspect and surface per-turn errors (throw or propagate) before E2 extraction and E4 selection consume the harness. Also (nits from same review): drop the unnecessary optional-usage guard, and include SDKResultError.errors[] in the thrown message instead of just the subtype string.
close_reason: Per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error now throws immediately (first-error-wins, named error type, no secrets); non-success final result includes SDKResultError.errors[]; dead usage guard dropped. TDD, all 4 gates green. Commit 481cd7f on build/phase-1-foundation.

title: DECISION: planner tuning params (vegFloorK, untestedRate, time-penalty)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Concrete values for vegFloorK, untestedRate, and total-time penalty weighting. DATA-DEPENDENT: tuned during the plan-quality validation pass (T4 tune) on the real corpus; code ships with config defaults until then. Gates T4 tune.
design: PROPOSED RESOLUTION (draft, awaiting ratification) — 8zs.6 evidence: current defaults HOLD once retrieval is quality-aware + multi-seed. vegFloorK=2, untestedRate=0.15, fanoutMultiplier=4 produce a full, correctly-composed pool (weeknight 16 rated + ~15% untested + 3 rated-veg) and a dry-run of 5/6 rated meals with 1 intentional try-this. NO total-time-penalty weighting needed — the active_max hard filter + rated base already yield fast, quality weeknight picks. The earlier pool problems were a RETRIEVAL defect (quality-blind single-seed), not a param-tuning gap, so no knob changes are required. Revisit only if real weekly plans show drift.

title: DECISION: seed-query strategy (single vs category-seeded multi-query)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Pool diversity: a single generic diversity seed vs category/cuisine-seeded multi-query. DATA-DEPENDENT: pick only if v1.0 pools come back homogeneous (a tuning knob, not a redesign). Gates T4 tune.
design: PROPOSED RESOLUTION (draft, awaiting ratification) — 8zs.6 evidence: CATEGORY-SEEDED MULTI-QUERY, not single generic seed. A single bland seed ('varied family weeknight and weekend dinners') under-recalls: only ~2 rated dinners / 0 rated-veg among its top-48 neighbours despite 206 rated / 49 rated-veg in corpus. Resolution: DEFAULT_SEEDS = 6 category seeds (vegetarian family dinner; chicken dinner; beef or pork main dish; fish or seafood dinner; pasta/noodle/grain bowl; curry/stir-fry/braise), one explicitly vegetarian. composePools retrieves a quality-aware cluster per seed, merges per-seed-capped. Result: weeknight pool 3->19, veg&rated 0->3. Seed wording remains tunable under 8zs.6.

title: DECISION: operator re-run affordance for a failed week
description: ADR 0002 open item. When a week goes 'failed' (mid-flight crash), define the manual re-run mechanism: a --force regenerate flag, delete-the-row, or a small 're-run week W' command. Gates the re-run task (T3 rerun). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: a 'bd-style' CLI subcommand on the daemon, e.g. 're-run <week_key> [--force]', that clears/overwrites the failed row and invokes generateForWeek with force=true. Reuses the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). Avoids raw row-deletion (error-prone) and keeps a clean audit. Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: failed-week recovery is a daemon CLI subcommand 're-run <week_key> [--force]' that clears/overwrites the failed row and calls generateForWeek(force=true), reusing the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). title: DECISION: active_max confidence threshold (fail-closed rule)
description: ADR 0001 open item. Pick the concrete time.confidence value below which a candidate FAILS active_max (fail-closed → not weeknight-eligible). DATA-DEPENDENT: resolved during the extraction-quality validation pass (T2 valid) against the real corpus — do not guess now. Gates T2 search + T2 valid.
close_reason: Ratified via q95.6 real-corpus validation (764 recipes): CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.5. Evidence — time.confidence tracks note time-explicitness not estimate error; danger cluster at conf<=0.2 (multi-day cures); 0.5 sits above it at a natural distribution break, preserving 547 weeknight-eligible (>> ~6/wk needed). search.ts doc comment updated to cite the validation. title: Own API workspace/key + low-spend email alert
description: New Anthropic API key in its own workspace (independent visibility/revocability); metered per-token (subscription cap does NOT catch this). Low-spend email alert at a threshold well below alarming (a few $/month is a genuine anomaly). SPEC §9.3.
notes:
OPS CHECKLIST (human action in Anthropic Console — I cannot do this; bead stays OPEN until you confirm). Drafted 2026-07-18.

1. Create a dedicated Workspace for Meal-Planner (Console → Settings → Workspaces). Isolates visibility, spend, and revocability from your other Anthropic usage (SPEC §9.3).
2. In that workspace, mint a new API key (sk-ant-…). Store the VALUE in the 1Password Meal-Planner vault, item `anthropic-api`, field `credential` — the op:// ref (MP_OP_ANTHROPIC_KEY_REF) already points there, so only the value changes. See RUNBOOK §3 "Anthropic API key" rotation steps (added in ws3/PR #22).
3. Set a low-spend usage alert on the workspace (Console → Usage/Billing). Steady-state spend is ~$0.04/successful weekly run (≈ $0.2/month), so an alert at ~$2–3/month flags a genuine anomaly (e.g. a re-extraction storm or runaway loop). NOTE: Anthropic offers ALERTS ONLY, not enforced per-key cutoffs — the real ceiling is the code-side generationDollarCap (fkg.2, enforced by meteredLlmClient; hardened in fkg.6/PR #18).
4. Restart the daemon so it loads the new key at boot (secrets load once at boot). Verify with a dev dry-run: MP_PROFILE=dev MP_POST_MODE=dry-run MP_FIRE_ON_START=1 — a bad key fails loudly there without touching prod. Then revoke any old key.

When done: bd close meal-planner-fkg.4.
close_reason: Done (user-confirmed): dedicated Meal-Planner Anthropic workspace + new API key minted, stored in 1Password Meal-Planner/anthropic-api/credential (op:// ref unchanged), low-spend usage alert set. Key rotation verified end-to-end via loadSecrets(op read)→Agent SDK→runQuery (returned OK, ~3s). Code-side generationDollarCap remains the enforced per-run ceiling.

title: Extraction-quality validation on real corpus
description: Run the extraction pass on the actual recipe corpus; eyeball confidence/needs_review; pick the concrete active_max confidence threshold for the fail-closed rule. Build-time validation, not a design unknown. ADR 0001 consequences/open items.
notes:
Real-corpus extraction validation (764 recipes, food-only index, extractor_version=2):

QUALITY: 764/764 extracted, 0 needs_review, 0 failures. Extraction pass is clean on the real corpus.

ACTIVE-TIME PRESENCE: 657 have a non-null time.active; 107 null (78 of those sit at conf<0.3 — correctly low-confidence, and null-active fails the gate regardless of threshold).

CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION (key finding): confidence tracks how EXPLICITLY the source note stated a time, NOT how wrong the estimate is. Sampled [0.3,0.5)-band recipes (Acqua Pazza 25m, curried chicken salad 10m, baked potato soup 30m) have perfectly sound active times — the model inferred from steps at moderate confidence. So a HIGH threshold would drop hundreds of good weeknight recipes as false negatives for no accuracy gain.

DANGER CLUSTER: genuinely unreliable estimates cluster at conf<=0.2 (e.g. Cured Arctic Char active=30/total=4320 — a multi-day cure; Farro total=2700). These are exactly what a fail-closed floor must exclude; a 0.5 gate excludes them.

THRESHOLD SENSITIVITY (weeknight-eligible = active present, active<=60, conf>=T):
  T=0.3 -> 634 | 0.4 -> 580 | 0.5 -> 547 | 0.6 -> 425 | 0.7 -> 298
Marginal drops: 0.3->0.4 -54, 0.4->0.5 -33, 0.5->0.6 -122 (CLIFF), 0.6->0.7 -127.
A large distribution mode sits in [0.5,0.7); 0.5 is a natural break just above the low-confidence danger cluster while preserving abundant supply (547 >> ~6 needed/week).

RECOMMENDATION for b7n: ratify CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.5 (confirms the current interim default as evidence-backed; no code change to the value).
close_reason: Ratified via q95.6 real-corpus validation (764 recipes): CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.5. Evidence — time.confidence tracks note time-explicitness not estimate error; danger cluster at conf<=0.2 (multi-day cures); 0.5 sits above it at a natural distribution break, preserving 547 weeknight-eligible (>> ~6/wk needed). search.ts doc comment updated to cite the validation. Extraction pass itself clean: 764/764, 0 needs_review, 0 failures.

title: fireOnStart + already-live row (resumeQuietly path) can reproduce the 8o3 PK-collision/misleading-alert in dev
description: Follow-up from 8o3 (PR #42). The 8o3 fix neutralizes 'force' for the one same-week onTrigger that follows an onStartup CATCH-UP this boot (empty-row case). But if a dev daemon boots with an ALREADY-LIVE session row from a prior boot (onStartup takes the resumeQuietly path, not catch-up) AND MP_FIRE_ON_START=1, fireOnStart still fires onTrigger for that week with forceRegenerate=true -> plain store.insert collides with the pre-existing row -> same 'UNIQUE constraint failed: session.week_key' + misleading 'test-fire failed' alert. Not covered by 8o3's fix (its suppression only arms after a catch-up generate this boot). Lower priority: reboots of an already-planned dev week are less common than the go-live empty-boot flow, but it's the same symptom operators were told not to worry about.
design: Extend the boot-scoped suppression to also arm when onStartup resolves a same-week live row via resumeQuietly (not only after catch-up generate), OR route fireOnStart through the operator force-overwrite path (rerun.ts delete-then-generate) instead of a plain force-insert. Confirm the genuine stale-'generating' alert still fires.

title: Re-add safe whitespace normalization to note-body reader (post-#20, no tag stripping)
description: Found during go-live verification 2026-07-20. Commit #20 (q95.16) correctly removed stripHtml (its /<[^>]*>/ regex was deleting real recipe text like 'simmer <10 min'), but stripHtml ALSO did useful whitespace cleanup (trim each line + drop blank lines) that was lost. Web-pasted recipe notes (JXA plaintext) now carry raw tabs/blank-line noise into embeddings + extraction. Re-add ONLY the whitespace normalization — trim each line, drop empty lines — WITHOUT any angle-bracket/tag stripping and WITHOUT HTML-entity decoding (plaintext has neither). Apply in notes-reader.readNotes so embed + extraction + contentHash all see the same normalized body. MUST preserve <...> recipe text (the #20 regression intent). Bonus: because the OLD stripHtml produced this same trimmed/blank-dropped output for notes without <...>, re-adding it should make the current stored index (built pre-#20) match again for those notes — likely shrinking the pending one-time re-sync from 694 recipes to just those containing <...> spans.
design: Add normalizeNoteBody(body): split on \n, trim each line, drop empty lines, join with \n. Apply to note.body in readNotes. Update the #20 regression test: assert <...> CONTENT survives while surrounding whitespace is normalized (no longer byte-for-byte verbatim). Out of scope: collapsing intra-line whitespace runs, stripping U+FFFC image-placeholder chars (note as possible follow-ups).
close_reason: Implemented + merged via PR #39 (commit 2f07b7a); safe whitespace normalization re-added to note-body reader, #20 angle-bracket invariant preserved, 633 tests green.

title: Record token usage from failed runQuery calls (currently unmetered spend)
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. agent-sdk-client.ts throws mid-stream after turns have already billed (:104-106, :156-165); metered-llm-client.ts records only on success (documented as deliberate). Rate-limit/overloaded failures after full prompt ingestion cost real input tokens the meter never sees — and since sync retries needs_review notes every pass (q95.7), a persistently failing corpus burns dollars invisible to the cap. Combined with fkg.6, sync failure paths are entirely budget-invisible today.
acceptance_criteria: AgentSdkLlmClient attaches accumulated usage to the thrown error (custom error type carrying usage); meteredLlmClient records it in catch/finally before rethrowing; cap still errs conservative; tests cover a mid-run failure being metered.
notes: Related: fkg.5 (cache-aware rates) touches the same LlmResult.usage plumbing — consider doing together.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #29: LlmCallError carries partial usage from a mid-stream throw; meteredLlmClient records it once in catch before rethrow so failed-call spend counts against the cap. fkg.6 gates intact. Verified 555 tests + CI green.

title: Per-note size cap on the extraction prompt
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. extraction.ts:58-96 interpolates the full note body into the prompt; notes-reader.ts tolerates up to 64 MB total. One pathological note (pasted article, hundreds of KB of plaintext) produces an enormous extraction call — and a possibly larger repair call quoting the previous response — before any cost accounting reacts (cap check is post-call, fkg.6). Hash-gating makes it recur on every edit of that note.
acceptance_criteria: Body passed to buildExtractionPrompt capped (e.g. first 15-20 KB with a truncation marker — comfortably covers any real recipe), or oversized notes skipped-and-flagged needs_review without an LLM call; test with an oversized body.
notes: Companion to q95.7 (retry cap) and fkg.6 (pre-call cap check).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #30: MAX_EXTRACTION_BODY_CHARS=16000 cap + capBody() truncation marker at the single prompt-assembly point; bounds initial + (transitively) repair calls. Verified 557 tests + CI green.

title: Pool-sufficiency pre-check before the selection call
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18. Empty/thin pools (fresh index, tag wipe q95.13, over-selective filter combo) make the exact slot counts unsatisfiable, yet build-plan.ts:89-109 still issues the selection call AND the repair call — two guaranteed-doomed paid calls, then a misleading PlanValidationError (expected 4 constrained, found 0) instead of naming the real problem.
acceptance_criteria: Before the LLM call: weeknight pool >= constrained slots, weekend pool >= relaxed slots, and distinct-id sufficiency across both; throw a dedicated InsufficientPoolError naming pool sizes (flows to the existing failure path/alert). Tests for each insufficiency.
notes: index.ts:205-209 already acknowledges the empty-index case; this generalizes it and saves the doomed spend.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #37: assertPoolsSufficient pre-check (InsufficientPoolError) before the LLM call in buildPlan; three distinct-id inequalities (weeknight, weekend, union) — saves doomed selection+repair spend, clear message. Spy-verified no LLM call on doomed runs. Verified 597 tests + CI green.

title: Daemon/config hardening nits: resolved-path dev/prod guard, per-week dry-run ts, process-level rejection handlers
description: From robustness review 2026-07-18, three small hardening items. (1) profile.ts:110-117 validates dev-vs-prod SQLite path separation by raw string comparison — data/mp.sqlite vs ./data/mp.sqlite passes while resolving to the same file, exactly the collision SPEC 7 exists to prevent (dev force-regenerate could then overwrite prod rows); compare path.resolve() of both. (2) index.ts:65-79 buildDryRunPost counter is per-process, so dev rows accumulate duplicate thread_ts=dryrun-1 across boots, breaking the getByThreadTs reverse map the rows are retained for; use dryrun-${week_key}. (3) No process.on(unhandledRejection/uncaughtException) safety net: a stray rejection off the awaited chain crashes silently (err.log only) and KeepAlive masks flapping; install handlers that append to the local log and best-effort alert before a deliberate exit.
acceptance_criteria: All three fixed with tests where practical (path-resolve guard and dry-run ts are unit-testable).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #34: path.resolve() dev/prod SQLite guard; week-scoped dryrun-${week_key} ts; makeFatalHandler (sync log + bounded best-effort alert + deliberate exit(1)) on unhandledRejection/uncaughtException. Verified 576 tests + CI green.

title: Pair a side dish with a main (e.g. burgers + potato salad)
description: q95.11 excludes #side recipes from being standalone dinners and captures is_side on candidates. Next: optionally PAIR a side with a compatible main in the plan (the user's real use — 'we make this with burgers'). Design: a side pool + a pairing step (LLM or heuristic) that attaches an optional side to selected mains; schema/WeekPlan additions for a paired side; render + grocery implications. Data (is_side) already captured.
design:
PROPOSAL (drafted 2026-07-19 — NOT ratified; awaiting review).

RECOMMENDATION: Attach an optional side INSIDE the existing single selection call (no 2nd LLM call), sourced from a new bounded `sides` pool from composePools; add optional `side:{recipe_id,title}` on SelectedMealSchema (distinct from veg.second_dish) + `sideRecipe` on EnrichedMeal; validate/render like the existing second_dish path; ship as v1.x additive, with grocery (v4, bd 0za) a downstream consumer only.

1. MECHANISM — LLM inline in the one existing call. A heuristic is a dead end: the corpus has NO cuisine tags (tag-metadata.ts yields only course/quality/season/effort/veg), so no affinity to compute; "burgers→potato salad" is world-knowledge the model has. A 2nd pairing call breaks ADR-0003's <=2-call budget (selectValidatedPlan's one shared repair, 8zs.10). So render a Sides sub-section in buildSelectionPrompt (input.ts) and let the model optionally attach a side per meal; the existing repair loop covers side violations free. Trade-off: prompt grows one candidate block (more tokens, one call) vs a whole extra call.

2. SIDE POOL — one new bounded search_recipes call, carried as Pools.sides. Sides are structurally excluded from mains pools today (main_dinner_only:true, gated by tm.main_dinner_eligible). search.ts has no positive side selector — add `sides_only?:boolean` (keep-only tm.is_side, mutually exclusive with main_dinner_only). Add one side search in composePools, deduped, exposed as Pools.sides.
   HUMAN DECISION (a): a side is an accompaniment to the shared main for EVERYONE (distinct from second_dish = a veg substitute-main just for the daughter). The main's own veg path already covers her; must the SIDE also be veg-satisfiable so she can eat it too? Recommend YES (validate side candidate veg_status) — flagged.

3. SCHEMA — new optional `side`, do NOT reuse second_dish (different meaning; can co-occur). SelectedMealSchema (select.ts): `side: z.object({recipe_id:z.string(), title:z.string()}).strict().optional()`; EnrichedMealSchema (enrich.ts): `sideRecipe: RecipeSchema.optional()`. Additive: strict schemas gain the field to ACCEPT it; the lenient resume read (resume.ts .passthrough(), bd6.13) already tolerates it → live-week resume survives with no change.

4. WHICH MEALS / HOW MANY — optional per-meal, LLM discretion, small weekly cap (config e.g. maxPairedSides:2); attach only when a main "wants" one (occasional, not nightly).
   HUMAN DECISION (b): always-pair-when-tagged vs LLM-discretion+cap — recommend discretion+cap. (c): the cap value.

5. RENDER + GROCERY — a "+ side: <title>" line in render.ts mirroring renderVegCoverage's second_dish branch. Grocery: sideRecipe carries the full ingredient block (like recipe/secondDishRecipe), so v4 reconciliation (bd 0za) aggregates it exactly as the second dish — flag the dependency, OUT OF SCOPE here.

6. PHASING — v1.x additive. Touches only selection + render; needs neither v2.0 day-assignment nor v4.0 grocery; fits v1.0's unordered slot-typed set (a side attaches to a main, not a day). Build after the core planner is stable.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH (order, when ratified): 1. search.ts sides_only filter. 2. pools.ts Pools.sides + bounded side search + config maxPairedSides/flag. 3. select.ts add `side` to schema + prompt output-shape doc. 4. input.ts Sides candidate sub-section + RULES clause (optional, <=cap, from side pool, veg-satisfiable). 5. validate.ts side membership in pools.sides, veg-consistency, <=cap, include side ids in checkNoDuplicates. 6. enrich.ts fetch/attach sideRecipe when meal.side present. 7. render.ts "+ side:" line. 8. resume.ts no change (passthrough) — confirm in a test. 9. note bd 0za (v4 grocery) as the sideRecipe consumer.

FLAGGED HUMAN DECISIONS: (a) must a paired side be veg-satisfiable for the daughter? (b) always-pair-when-tagged vs LLM-discretion+cap; (c) the cap value. To proceed: resolve (a)/(b)/(c), ratify, then dispatch an implementation subagent.
notes: RATIFIED 2026-07-19 by Jonathan. (a) YES — a paired side MUST be veg-satisfiable (validate side candidate veg_status; sides are almost-universally vegetarian anyway, so low cost). (b) LLM discretion (optional per-meal), not always-pair-when-tagged. (c) maxPairedSides = 2 (hard ceiling; the prompt should steer toward 0-1 typically — 'usually one at most'). Implement per the design-field 9-step sketch. Additive v1.x feature.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #38 (ratified): optional veg side attached inline in the one selection call; sides_only search filter, Pools.sides, MP_MAX_PAIRED_SIDES (default 2), side field on SelectedMeal + sideRecipe on canonical EnrichedMeal, checkSides (in-pool/vegetarian/<=cap + dedup) through the single-repair path, distinct render line, resume tolerated via passthrough. 8zs.10/8zs.11/8zs.12 undisturbed. Verified 626 tests + CI green.

title: CostMeter: apply cache-aware input rates (cache-read cheaper than fresh input)
description: Post-q95.10, agent-sdk-client counts all input categories (input_tokens + cache_creation + cache_read) at the single inputPerMTok rate — a deliberate conservative over-estimate for the cost cap. For more accurate accounting/reporting, model the real Anthropic rates: cache-write ~1.25x input, cache-read ~0.1x input. Requires threading the cache-token breakdown through LlmResult.usage (currently flattened to inputTokens/outputTokens) into CostMeter. Low priority: current behavior is safe (never undercounts) and cache reads are rare across distinct notes.
acceptance_criteria: CostMeter cost reflects cache-write/cache-read rate tiers; LlmResult.usage carries the cache breakdown; cap still errs conservative.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #32: LlmUsage split into fresh/cacheWrite/cacheRead; tieredCostUsd prices fresh 1x / cache-write 1.25x / cache-read 0.1x; CostTotals.inputTokens still sums all three. Cap still conservative. Verified 564 tests + CI green.

title: Promote EnrichedWeekPlanSchema into enrich.ts (dedupe from resume.ts)
description: From resume-quietly review (bd6.5, commit 7502d37): resume.ts composes an EnrichedWeekPlanSchema/EnrichedMealSchema (from SelectedMealSchema + RecipeSchema) locally because enrich.ts exports no schema. It's correct and anchored to real types, but is a second independently-maintained description of EnrichedWeekPlan that will drift if EnrichedMeal/EnrichedWeekPlan change. Promote the schema into enrich.ts (its owner) as the single source of truth and have resume.ts (+ any v3.0 listener) import it. Low priority; do when a second consumer appears.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #33: enrich.ts owns canonical EnrichedMeal/EnrichedWeekPlanSchema (single source of truth, types inferred); resume.ts imports + layers lenient read variant preserving bd6.13. No cycle. Verified 567 tests + CI green.

title: generateForWeek robustness follow-ups (failed-write defense, force+existing-row, error sanitization)
description: Deferred Low findings from generateForWeek review (bd6.3, commit 09184a3). (1) The pre-post failure path's own transition-to-failed write is undefended: if that update throws, the original buildPlan/post error is lost and alert() never fires — consider guarding it. (2) force+existing-row: expirePriorIfUncommitted runs and can mutate the prior week before store.insert throws the PK constraint — bd6.6 (operator re-run) owns clean force-overwrite; add a test/comment when it lands. (3) alert()/error messages forward raw error text from buildPlan/post (E4/E5) — state an explicit no-secret contract for those implementations, or sanitize at the alert boundary. All Low; non-blocking.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #31: failed-write guard (log secondary, still alert+throw original), force+existing-row documented+tested (bd6.6 owns overwrite), no-secret contract on BuildPlanFn/PostFn. ADR-0002 invariants preserved. Verified 559 tests + CI green.

title: Optional: bounded retry-before-fail for transient LLM-call stalls (qjk follow-up)
description: Follow-up from qjk (PR #43). The per-call timeout currently fails fast to the failed+alert path on the first wedged call. qjk's design floated a small bounded retry for TRANSIENT stalls before failing (a brief API rough patch may clear on a fresh subprocess). Deferred to keep qjk surgical. Consider: N bounded retries with fresh abortController each, still << trigger watchdog, tests with fake timers. Low priority — fail-fast + alert already prevents the silent 45-min hang.
